data_in.description: task_002.expected_outputs[0]: qc_summary data.table with rows corresponding to input compounds and columns: compound ID, status (indicating which filter each ion failed or if the ion passed all applied filters)
Filtered mpactr object (data_filtered) containing peak table and filter metadata
qc_summary() output: data.table with compound IDs and ion status (passed/failed filters)

data_out.description: Treemap PNG file showing ion counts and percentages grouped by filter status (mispicked, group, replicability, insource, passed)

domain_knowledge: Ion status categories in mpactr include mispicked (isotopic pattern splitting errors), group (solvent/media blank contamination), replicability (non-reproducible across technical replicates), insource (fragment ions created during ionization), and passed (ions meeting all filter criteria).
The qc_summary() function in mpactr returns a data.table where each row represents an input ion and status column indicates which filter(s) the ion failed or if it passed all filters.
Treemaps display hierarchical data where rectangle area is proportional to ion count and color intensity (Greens palette) represents relative abundance or importance within each status category.
Reference semantics in R6 objects used by mpactr enable in-place data updates without copying, making memory-efficient chaining of filters and downstream analysis operations.
Ion filtering workflow typically proceeds in order: mispicked ions → blank contamination → replicability → insource fragments, allowing high-quality ions to be identified before downstream analysis.

expert_review: treemap visual structure matches vignette exemplar — Greens palette applied, geom_treemap() and geom_treemap_text() layers present, legend absent as specified
expert_review: treemap aesthetic and layout match the vignette reference (Greens color scheme, text labels readable, tile proportions reflect per-status ion percentages correctly)
per-status ion counts and percentages computed from qc_summary() data.table are numerically consistent with source filtering pipeline state

evidence_snippets.text: [results] How can filtering results from qc_summary() be visualized as a treemap showing the count and percentage of ions in each filter status category?: 'You can visualize filtering results with a tree map using the filtering summary obtained from `qc_summary()` and the packages `ggplot2` and `treemapify`.'

[results] Ion counts and percentages by status are computed from qc_summary() output using data.table syntax, then rendered as a treemap with geom_treemap() and geom_treemap_text() to display status labels, ion counts, and percentages; the visualization can be customized with ggplot2 scale_fill_brewer() and theme() functions.: 'ion_counts <- qc_summary(data_filtered)[, .(count = .N), by = status][, percent := (count / sum(count) * 100)]

Finally, we plot the treemap:

tm <- ggplot(ion_counts) +
  aes(area = percent, fill ='

[methods] Filtered mpactr object (data_filtered) containing peak table and filter metadata: 'Overall, `r nrow(get_peak_table(data_filtered))` ions remain in the feature table. A summary of the filtering, as a tree map is below'

[methods] qc_summary() output: data.table with compound IDs and ion status (passed/failed filters): 'This function returns a `data.table` reporting the ion status for each input ion. This includes which filter each ion failed or passed, or if the ion passed all applied filters.'
[methods] Treemap PNG file showing ion counts and percentages grouped by filter status (mispicked, group, replicability, insource, passed): 'A summary of the filtering, as a tree map is below'
[methods] R: 'This table can be used for a variety of analyses that can be conducted in R'
[methods] ggplot: 'creating an interactive plot of input features and the filters they failed, if any, using `ggplot` and `plotly`'

cmd: library(mpactr); library(ggplot2); library(treemapify); library(data.table); data_filtered <- readRDS('{inputs.mpactr_filtered_object}'); qc_dt <- data_filtered@qc_summary; status_counts <- qc_dt[, .N, by=status]; status_counts[, pct := round(100*N/sum(N), 1)]; p <- ggplot(status_counts, aes(area=N, fill=status, label=paste0(status, '\n', N, ' (', pct, '%)'))) + geom_treemap() + geom_treemap_text(colour='white', place='centre', size=12) + scale_fill_brewer(palette='Greens', type='seq') + theme(legend.position='none') + labs(title='Ion Status Distribution'); ggsave('treemap_qc_status.png', plot=p, width=10, height=8, dpi=300); cat('Treemap saved to treemap_qc_status.png\n')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Treemap PNG file showing ion counts and percentages grouped by filter status (mispicked, group, replicability, insource, passed)
finding: Ion counts and percentages by status are computed from qc_summary() output using data.table syntax, then rendered as a treemap with geom_treemap() and geom_treemap_text() to display status labels, ion counts, and percentages; the visualization can be customized with ggplot2 scale_fill_brewer() and theme() functions.

task_description: Extract per-status ion counts and percentages from qc_summary() data, then render a treemap visualization using geom_treemap() with the Greens color palette. Save the treemap as a PNG file.

task_inputs.label: Filtered mpactr object with qc metadata

task_objective: Compute ion counts and percentages by filter status using qc_summary() and data.table, then render and save a treemap visualization with geom_treemap, Greens palette, and no legend as a PNG/PDF file.

task_outputs.label: Treemap PNG visualization of ion filter status

title: Reproduce treemap visualisation of filtering QC using qc_summary(), ggplot2, and treemapify

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Treemap PNG file showing ion counts and percentages grouped by filter status (mispicked, group, replicability, insource, passed).
workflow_description: 1. Load filtered mpactr object and extract qc_summary() data.table reporting ion status (passed/failed filters). 2. Aggregate ion counts by status category and calculate percentage of total ions per status. 3. Create a treemap using ggplot2 geom_treemap() and geom_treemap_text() with Greens palette, no legend, sized by ion count and labeled with status and percentage. 4. Save the rendered treemap to PNG file.
